result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the change:

## Root Cause

The `CommentWrappingRule` in `CommentWrappingRule.kt:54` was incorrectly flagging block comments inside single-line blocks (lambdas) like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` with the error "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed". The rule detected elements on both sides of the block comment (whitespace before `{` and after `}`) but didn't account for the legitimate case where a block comment is the sole content of a block.

## Fix

Added a check in `CommentWrappingRule.kt:54-61` that skips the error when all three conditions are met:
1. The previous code leaf (skipping whitespace/comments) is `LBRACE` (`{`)
2. The next code leaf (skipping whitespace/comments) is `RBRACE` (`}`)
3. The block comment is single-line (doesn't contain `\n`)

This allows patterns like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` to pass without lint violations, while still flagging genuinely problematic patterns like `val foo /* comment */ = "foo"`.

## File Changed

- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt` — Added imports for `LBRACE`, `RBRACE`, `prevCodeLeaf`, `nextCodeLeaf`, and added the early-return condition for block comments inside single-line blocks.
